feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Application Studio
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Application Studio Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Fiori Elements: Change Column width
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

what is the easiest way to change the column width in a Fiori Elements app? In BAS there is a function to change the column width, but it does not seem to adjust it.

regards,

Lars

entry.title: Logout functionality SAP CAP application deployed in Cloud Foundry using Managed approuter
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have built a SAP CAP application with UI5 and used managed approuter. The application is deployed in Cloud Foundry and I can access it through HTML5 applications. This application uses XSUAA authentication. 

I want to implement logout functionality in this application for the user. I have read many blogs and I'm unable to implement it. I have done things like making changes in xs-app.json file to include the logout parameter as described in the link:

https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/logout

On the logout button in my app, i put the controller logic to 

window.location.replace("/my/logout").

After this when I click on logout, it takes me to a URL:
"https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/resources/sap-ui-logout-url" and when i click the back button, it again goes directly into the application logged in as the previous user, which means that it is not deleting the session maybe.

I even followed the question:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/logout-sap-ui5-app-cloud-foundry-sample-code/qaq-p/12248689

(Yes, I gave relative path in the controller script like my/logout as I am using managed approuter)

But when i do this, i get an internal server error after clicking on logout.

On checking the error logs of the app, I see the error as:
GET request to /74cf1c07-1827-49d2-9bc9-90ba4d9f6781.capleavemanagementappservice.leavemanagement-1.0.0/my/logout completed with status 500 request method (GET) does not match logout configuration logoutMethod (POST)


Does anybody know how can I implement a logout functionality in my application?

Thanks,
Raihan

entry.title: How to filter the list when the column is 1:n relation
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to create a webapp using SAP Business Application Studio in BTP. 

The app is connected to S4 HANA Cloud system using oData v2 API

I have view which is a list of products. I want to add a search box to filter the result by Production Description column. Production and Product Description has 1:n relation.

I am using the below source codes. But it doesn't work. 

I get the error in the console - Error during request to remote service: Left hand expression of memberaccess operator has wrong cardinality (to many not allowed)

Can someone please help me?

 

                var aTableSearchState =[];
                var sQuery = oEvent.getParameter("query");

                if (sQuery && sQuery.length > 0) {
                    var ProductTypeFilter = new Filter("ProductType", FilterOperator.Contains, sQuery);
                    var MaterialFilter = new Filter("Material", FilterOperator.Contains, sQuery);
                    var LanguageFilter = new Filter("to_Description/Language", FilterOperator.EQ, 'EN');
                    var DescriptionFilter = new Filter("to_Description/Description", FilterOperator.Contains, sQuery);
                    var QueryFilter1 = new Filter({ filters:[LanguageFilter, DescriptionFilter], and: true});
                    //var QueryFilter = new Filter({ filters:[ProductTypeFilter, MaterialFilter], and: false});
                    var QueryFilter = new Filter({ filters:[ProductTypeFilter, MaterialFilter, QueryFilter1], and: false});
                    aTableSearchState.push(QueryFilter);
                }
                this._applySearch(aTableSearchState);
 
         
_applySearch: function(aTableSearchState) {
          
           var oTable = this.byId("table"),
           oViewModel = this.getModel("worklistView");
            oTable.getBinding("items").filter(aTableSearchState, "Application");
            // changes the noDataText of the list in case there are no filter results
            if (aTableSearchState.length !== 0) {
                oViewModel.setProperty("/tableNoDataText", this.getResourceBundle().getText("worklistNoDataWithSearchText"));
            }

        }

entry.title: SSO not working. OAuth2UserTokenExchange. Unable to login to travel list app
entry.content:
Hi Experts!

Recently I deployed an app to HTML5 Repository in SAP BTP

html5 app repository:

when i click on "sapfedemotravellist"  , i am presented with an alert in the new tab:

even after putting correct id and password this just keeps on coming. I tried with client id and client secret also and still the same alert keep popping.

Please help me resolve this issue.

Destination:

Destination Trust:

Trial Users:

Subaccount Trial: Trust Configuration:

Subscriptions & Instances:

App's xs-security.json

{
  "xsappname": "sapfedemotravellist",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "description": "Security profile of called application",
  "scopes": [],
  "role-templates": []
}
 
xs-app.json
 
{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/sap/(.*)$",
      "target": "/sap/$1",
      "destination": "abap-cloud-default_abap-trial-########trial-dev",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": false
    },
    {
      "source": "^/resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/test-resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/test-resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}

Please help in this regard.

@SAPSupport 

@jlong 

@K120525 

@dennis_koehler 

@DonWilliams 

@paula_augedahl 

@Sandra_Rossi 

@William_Wu 

@Ulrich_Schmidt1 

@sapcommunity

entry.title: Business application studio: DevSpace stucks in "Starting", Timeout
entry.content:
Hello All, 

my DevSpace stucks in "Starting" phase and runs in a timeout after a while. I think it's because its bigger than 6GB. 

I really need the data from this space. I tried a lot of stuff (start/stop, invisible mode, refresh cache etc.), but nothing works. 

Can someone support?

Thanks.

KR

Bettina

entry.title: Can I build an Overview Page (OVP) with JSON data ?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope you're doing well. I have a question regarding SAP Fiori and SAP Business Application Studio (BAS).

I'm currently working on a custom application, and I would like to build an Overview Page (OVP). However, instead of using a traditional OData service with annotations (as OVP typically requires), I want to use static or dynamic JSON data as my data source.

My goals are:

  • To build an OVP-style UI (with cards/tiles displaying KPIs or summary information).

  • To use local or remote JSON data (e.g., from a JSON file or API).

  • To develop and test this in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS), possibly using freestyle SAPUI5.

My questions are:

  1. Is it possible to build an Overview Page (OVP) using only JSON data (without an OData service)?

  2. If so, are there any best practices or examples on how to bind JSON data to the cards on the OVP?

  3. Can I use mock data (e.g., mockserver) in BAS for this purpose?

  4. Is it necessary to use Fiori Elements, or can I achieve this with a freestyle UI5 application?

Any guidance, sample projects, or documentation links would be really appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Fiori Elements List Report: How to enable the export to pdf option using BAS?
entry.content: Hello. Im currently creating a full stack CAP application in the BAS environment. When I create my list report using fiori elements, the only option I get to export my data is an XLSX file. Even when I choose the "export as" option, the only option given is the excel file. Is there a configuration to enable the PDF Export? Because I was looking at the official documentation but couldn't find any practical example for my development environment. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Enable selection mode in Fiori Elements
entry.content:
I developed a List Report app using Fiori Elements through RAP.

I wanted to enable the selection mode "Single" so I can single record and use a custom action to open a dialog for further processing.

I already added the table setting "selectionMode" into "Single" in the manifest.json but it doesnt reflect in the app.

When I run app and check the table's selection mode, it shows as undefined.

How can I enable the selectionMode "Single" using Fiori Elements?

entry.title: Fiori App Controller Extension: Overrides not being triggered successfully
entry.content:
I'm trying to make a simple controller extension, which would hook onto a method when user clicks a button. The name of this method is "onClickActionC_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTPHeader1."

I've tried the following code, and several variations, trying to get a simple console.log message, but I can't get any of the methods below to trigger. Is there something I'm missing in order to get any of the below methods to trigger after their respective base controller methods are called?

 

sap.ui.define(
    [
        'sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension',
        'sap/ui/core/mvc/OverrideExecution'
    ],

   
    function (
        ControllerExtension,
        OverrideExecution //,Dialog, Button, mobileLibrary
       
    ) {
        console.log("testing 1");
        "use strict";
       
        return ControllerExtension.extend("customer.zperform.maintenance.jobs.PopupMessage", {
            metadata: {
                methods: {
                    onclick: {
                        public: true,
                        final: false,
                        overrideExecution: "After",
                    },

                    onmousedown: {
                        public: true,
                        final: false,
                        overrideExecution: "After",
                    },

                    onClickActionC_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTPHeader1: {
                        public: true,
                        final: false,
                        overrideExecution: "After",
                    },
                }
            },
            override: {
                onclick: function (oEvent) {
                    console.log("testing 2");
                },

                onmousedown: function (oEvent) {
                    console.log("testing 2");
                },
               
                onClickActionC_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTPHeader1: function (oEvent) {
                    console.log("testing 2");
                }
            }
        });
    }
)
 
 
 
Manifest: 
{
"fileName": "manifest",
"layer": "CUSTOMER_BASE",
"fileType": "appdescr_variant",
"reference": "i2d.eam.perfmaintjobs.manages1",
"id": "customer.zperform.maintenance.jobs",
"namespace": "apps/i2d.eam.perfmaintjobs.manages1/appVariants/customer.zperform.maintenance.jobs/",
"version": "0.1.0",
"content": [
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
"content": {
"modelId": "i18n"
},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
"content": {
"modelId": "@i18n"
},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
"content": {
"modelId": "i18n|sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport|C_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTP"
},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/ListReport/C_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTP/i18n.properties"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
"content": {
"modelId": "i18n|sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage|C_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTP"
},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/ObjectPage/C_MaintenanceJobWorkItemTP/i18n.properties"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_addNewModelEnhanceWith",
"content": {
"modelId": "i18n|sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage|C_MaintenanceJobTP"
},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/ObjectPage/C_MaintenanceJobTP/i18n.properties"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_ui5_setMinUI5Version",
"content": {
"minUI5Version": "1.108.38"
}
},
{
"changeType": "appdescr_app_setTitle",
"content": {},
"texts": {
"i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties"
}
}
]
}

entry.title: Modify existing custom Fiori App
entry.content:
There is an already existing custom Fiori app using RAP deployed in the system (S4HPCE). I have a requirement to modify the UI by adding custom action. Previous developer dont have a copy of the BAS project.

My question is how can I load the project into BAS so I can modify the app and deploy?

entry.title: Deploying a Fiori Adaption Project application to the Fiori launchpad in public cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

for a customer project, we need to modify a standard fiori application (doubtful account validation), and deploy it to the public cloud. Currently i try to deploy the application without changes, because i want to have a working deployment process, before i change things in the app.

For the deployment i followed a tutorial that seems to fit my needs:
Link 

My deployment settings in BAS look different. So i was not able to adjust everything what was mentioned in the tutorial.

When i try to select the app in the PC in the "manage launchpad space" transaction, i simply cannot select it.

Has anyone experience in doing adaption projects and public cloud deployments?

Thanks in advance !

entry.title: Blog post still shows "Awaiting Review" after 2 days – request for moderation status
entry.content:
Hello SAP Moderation Team,

I submitted a blog titled “Build and Deploy a Full Stack SAP CAPM App with Fiori UI on SAP BTP Cloud” on Wednesday.
It is still showing as “Awaiting Review” in my dashboard.

Here is the URL that was generated:
https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/build-and-deploy-a-full-stack-sap-capm-app-with-fiori-ui-on-sap-btp-cloud/ba-p/14124971

Can you please check the status or let me know if action is required from my side?

Thanks & regards,
Madhan

entry.title: How to Create Dynamic virtual table in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
Hi,

Can anyone help me How to create Dynamic Virtual table in SAP Business Application Studio(BAS),

We are doing a replication project where we are moving data from source to HanaCloud, With the help of DP Agent & Adapters we created remote source & created virtual tables in BAS and we are moving data from virtual table to Hana Table.

Hence we are giving Hardcoded values while creating virtual table, But In Source the Table Name & Schema Name are different in Dev, Qa & Prod. Once I Created virtual table in dev the same has to move to QA through MTAR, We are not suppose to change anything in QA Manually and same to prod.

So, In this case How can I achieve creating virtual tables with different names in Dev, Qa and Prod?

I tried creating .hdbvirtualtableconfig and Environmental variables but couldn't achieve the solution, Please help me where I am missing Or If any other approach, Explain In-detail.

Please refer to the above screenshots I have created Environmental variables & Test1.hdbvirtualtableconfig, When I deploy Test1.hdbvirtualtableconfig,  virtual table is not created.

Thanks In Advance

entry.title: Error Deploying BSP App to S4 Hana Backend error message: No default virus profile active or found.
entry.content: Hi Community, I am trying to deploy BSP app to S4 HANA system via Business Application Studio, After entering Credentials I am getting the below error. how to resolve and deploy without any error.

entry.title: Issue with deployment of SAP UI5 application from BAS to BSP ABAP Repository.
entry.content:
Hi colleagues,

I'm unable to deploy UI5 application to ABAP Repository from Business Application Studio. I'm getting below errors:

Below is the deployment config:

Regards,

Saksham

entry.title: 404 not found error using the BTP destination service in SAP Business Application Studio
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I have created a multitarget application in which I have added the standalone app router and Basic Fiori Application.

I am using the xs-app JSON file in a Fiori application with the below getToken Destination Details.

I am utilising the destination details from xs-app.json in our controller.

when we are running our UI5 Application, we are getting a 404 Not Found response.

I still do not know the cause of the error. let me know what is the cause of the error and how to solve it?

entry.title: SAP CAP : 502 "Error during request to remote service: read ECONNRESET"
entry.content:
Hi,

I am developing SAP CAP application, node.js, trying to connect to external S4 onPrem service which is accessed via the cloud connector.

I am trying to reach S4 OData service from CAP Application locally in Business Application Studio via SAP Cloud Connector.

To connect to the destination and other services we are using the “cds bind -2” command, which created the .cdsrc-private.json file.

I am able to connect to the different BTP instances like the destination, authorization and connectivity instances, when i run the app using 

cds watch --profile hybrid

But when i'm trying to access any of the entities within the S4 OData service facing the following error,

{"error":{"code":"502","message":"Error during request to remote service: read ECONNRESET"}}

Has anyone faced this issue, please suggest an appropriate solution for this.

Also when the applicaiton is deployed to BTP,  the S4 odata services are accessiable via destination and the application is working as expected,

i want run the application locally for development phase, hence need your help,

Regards,

Aakash

entry.title: Delay data loading of SAP Macros table until button click? - UI5
entry.content:
Hi all - Wanted to know if its possible to delay loading of data from a macros table within a XML fragment? Currently, when you click on a list page record -> open object page -> my macros table in a custom section renders, and loads all table data correctly. All of this data is via CDS entity where I define the metaPath in my XML fragment. Table renders, and data loads in just fine. 

Now, I would like to extend the functionality by adding a button ("Get Data") in the object page custom section, that when clicked, will load the data, and not when user opens the object page. Anybody have any examples of this I can potentially reference? 

Right now, my initial thoughts were using the pre-made controller JS extension file for my object page to try to not bind the data in the onInit() function? Then in my controller file for the XML fragment load the frag, and bind the data upon button click?

 
sap.ui.define(
[
    "sap/m/MessageToast",
    'sap/ui/model/Filter',
    'sap/ui/model/FilterOperator',
    'sap/ui/core/Fragment'

], function(MessageToast, Filter, FilterOperator, Fragment) {
    'use strict';
    return {
        onLoadData: async function (oEvent) {
                  ***load fragment -> find macros table ID -> bind elements via metapath to entity?***
        }
    };
});

__PRESENT

__PRESENT__PRESENT

entry.title: SAP BAS Analytic List Page Error "aggregate based entities"?
entry.content:
Hi, 

i am trying to make my first Analytic List Page. I made the CDS Views and the Odata V4 Service. Now i am in the BAS and trying to Use the Floorplan and make the App. 

I have problems at the Point "Entity Selection", I tried to add different Annotations, but i still get the error: 

"The OData V4 service you have provided is not suitable for use in an Analytical List Page application. The service must contain aggregate based entities for this template."

Can someone help me or knows what i have to change? Regards Niels

entry.title: Get familiar with your first CAPM project using CDS graphic modeler
entry.content:
Getting Started with with your first CAMP project with CDS Graphical Modeler in SAP Business Application Studio.

As SAP developers, we frequently navigate the complexities of code, annotations, and metadata while constructing data models with Core Data Services (CDS). Although the capabilities of CDS are remarkable, handling intricate data models solely through code can be quite daunting particularly in extensive enterprise applications. This is where the CDS Graphical Modeler proves invaluable a revolutionary tool within SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) that enhances visual clarity and efficiency in CDS development.

What is CDS Graphical Modeler?

Instead of writing  .cds  files manually, we can now model our entities, relationships, and services visually, making it easier to understand and communicate your data architecture.

Lets create a dev space and then dive into the CAPM with cds.

Go to BTP COCKPIT Login 

First we need to logon to SAP Business Application Studio, you will see a list of the existing dev spaces:

you can use the existing devspace if it is preconfigured else create a devsapce.

 

 After you click on create devspace below screen will appear:

 

Enter a dev space name, and select "Full Stack Cloud Application" category, then click "Create Dev Space" button to create the dev space.

 

Wait until the newly created dev space is in "RUNNING" status:

 Now we can enter the dev space by clicking the dev space name:

Create a CAP Project


Now let’s create a sample CAP project using the project wizard. Click “Start from template” from the welcome page and launch the project wizard.

Please select “CAP Project” from the template in the project wizard:

Click “Start” button and enter the CAP project details in the next page of the project wizard. Please check “Basic Sample Files” and ensure the project wizard to generate sample CDS files for you.

 

Click “Finish” button to close the wizard and the dev space will refresh again and this time the newly CAP project folder will be in the project tree:

You can open db/data-model.cds in the project using the code editor:
